note: Phase 6 Task 10 -- AlternativeComparisonReport.ComputeAlternativeComparisonReport, the FINAL numeric compute of the port (with/without-project damage-reduction benefits via empirical-distribution subtraction). Both cases mirror AlternativeComparisonReportConsolidationTests.CreateAlternativeResults/ComputeResults exactly: a without-project and a with-project AlternativeResults, each built DIRECTLY (no AnnualizationCompute) with a base-year and future-year ScenarioResults holding one impact area with a Damage AggregatedConsequencesBinned and a LifeLoss AggregatedConsequencesBinned, each from a DynamicHistogram over Enumerable.Repeat(value, 100) (a deterministic constant-valued sample, so SampleMean == value exactly), then compute_alternative_comparison_report(without, [with]). BaseYear=2025, FutureYear=2075, PeriodOfAnalysis=50, ConvergenceCriteria(minIterations=1, maxIterations=1) (matches the C# test's `new(minIterations: 1, maxIterations: 1)`), ImpactAreaID=1, DamageCategory='residential', AssetCategory='structure', RiskType.Fail throughout (the AggregatedConsequencesBinned/StudyAreaConsequencesBinned::GetConsequenceResult real default, matching alternative.json's own established RiskType.Fail convention for this same reason). `reduction` case: without={base_damage:1000, future_damage:2000, base_lifeloss:10, future_lifeloss:20}, with={base_damage:300, future_damage:500, base_lifeloss:3, future_lifeloss:5} -- asserts SampleMeanBaseYearEADReduced(Damage)=1000-300=700, SampleMeanFutureYearEADReduced(Damage)=2000-500=1500, SampleMeanBaseYearEADReduced(LifeLoss)=10-3=7, SampleMeanFutureYearEADReduced(LifeLoss)=20-5=15 (mirrors BaseYearDamageReduced_ReturnsCorrectMean/FutureYearDamageReduced_ReturnsCorrectMean/BaseYearLifeLossReduced_ReturnsCorrectMean/FutureYearLifeLossReduced_ReturnsCorrectMean). `zero_reduction` case: without and with both {base_damage:500, future_damage:500, base_lifeloss:5, future_lifeloss:5} -- asserts both reduced means are 0 (mirrors ZeroReduction_WhenWithAndWithoutProjectAreEqual). `expected: "PIN"`, gate-captured from the real C# AlternativeComparisonReport/AlternativeComparisonReportResults (abs tol 0.1, matching the C# test's own `Assert.Equal(expected, actual, 1)` one-decimal-place literal) -- never hand-derived, though the 700/1500/7/15/0 values are exact algebraic subtraction of the constant-valued histogram means so the PIN is expected to reproduce them exactly.
